title: Skills Show
description: Before installing a skill, users may want to review its full content to understand what it does. The `specd skills show <name>` command prints the full markdown content of a named skill bundled in `@specd/skills`.
context: Before installing a skill, users may want to review its full content to understand what it does. The `specd skills show <name>` command prints the full markdown content of a named skill bundled in `@specd/skills`.

rules:
```
specd skills show <name> [--format text|json|toon]
```

- `<name>` — required positional; the name of the skill to display.
- `--format text|json|toon` — optional; output format, defaults to `text`.

In `text` mode (default), the command prints the raw markdown content of the skill file to stdout, preceded by a header line:

```
--- <name> ---
<markdown content>
```

In `json` or `toon` mode, the output is:

```json
{ "name": "...", "description": "...", "content": "..." }
```

Where `content` is the full markdown text of the skill.
If no skill with the given name exists in `@specd/skills`, the command exits with code 1 and prints an `error:` message to stderr.

constraints: The command is read-only — it never modifies any files
content in JSON/toon output is the verbatim file content, not parsed or transformed

scenarios.name: Missing name argument exits with usage error
when: specd skills show is run with no positional argument
then: AND stderr contains a usage error message

scenarios.name: Text output includes header and content
when: specd skills show my-skill is run
then: AND the full markdown content of the skill follows

scenarios.name: JSON output contains name, description, and content
when: specd skills show my-skill --format json is run
then: stdout is valid JSON with name, description, and content fields
AND content is the verbatim markdown text

scenarios.name: Unknown skill name exits with error
when: specd skills show nonexistent-skill is run
then: AND stderr contains an error: message
